title: Serum Creatinine and eGFR Equations
buzzwords: Byproduct of daily muscle breakdown
Freely filtered, not reabsorbed, minimally secreted
Value varies with sex, age, muscle mass
Plugged into equations to estimate filtration
Vegetarians and low muscle mass underestimate disease
SWAG mnemonic: sex, weight, age, gender
MDRD replaced by ethnicity-free 2021 update
Cockcroft-Gault, MDRD, and CKD-EPI

title: 24-Hour Creatinine Clearance
buzzwords: Direct timed measurement of solute clearance
Requires a full-day urine collection jug
Paired serum draw at midpoint of collection
Bypasses demographic-based estimating equations
Volume times urine concentration over plasma
Inaccurate if collection is missed or incomplete
Formula divides by 1440 minutes per day
CrCl = (UCr x V) / (PCr x 1440)

title: Blood Urea Nitrogen (BUN)
buzzwords: Serum assay reported as a nitrogen value
Rises with dehydration and high catabolism
Elevated after upper GI bleeding
Tracks confusion, asterixis, and pericarditis symptoms
Normal range roughly 7 to 20 mg/dL
Surrogate marker, not an estimate of filtration
Reflects uremic solute burden
Nitrogen portion of urea (BUN)

title: Urine Dipstick
buzzwords: Rapid bedside point-of-care screen
Colorimetric pads change shade by analyte
Reported semi-quantitatively from 0 to 4+
Estimates concentration via specific gravity 1.003-1.035
Catches glucose once threshold around 180 exceeded
Heme pad falsely positive in rhabdomyolysis
Detects leukocyte esterase and bacterial nitrites
Reagent strip for pH, protein, blood, glucose

title: Urine Microscopy (Sediment)
buzzwords: Centrifuged pellet examined on a slide
Reported per high-power field
Distinguishes dysmorphic from normal red cells
Identifies coffin-lid, envelope, and hexagonal crystals
Eosinophils suggest allergic interstitial nephritis
Muddy brown granular casts mark tubular necrosis
WBC casts localize infection to the kidney
RBC casts pathognomonic for glomerulonephritis

title: Proteinuria Quantification (UPCR, ACR, 24h)
buzzwords: Quantifies leak of plasma proteins into urine
Spot ratios use creatinine to normalize concentration
Daylong collection considered the most accurate method
Immunoassay variant detects microalbuminuria early
Screens for diabetic and hypertensive kidney damage
Turbidimetric ratio catches all protein types
Bence Jones light chains missed by dipstick and ACR
UPCR, ACR, and 24-hour urine protein
